tokens: Jim, could we yaw right a little bit and point that omni right at you?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Any words on that uplink, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Why don't you put in that yaw maneuver; yaw right 20 degrees, that might help.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And how about reading that up to me, and I'll copy it down.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Looks like we're getting good data now, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think we'll start the...John says we'll start the PDI from zero yaw since the omni is pointing right at you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: How do you read us VOX, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Read loud and clear on VOX.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let's check the DPS configuration card.
(Reading settings John will check) CB(11): Deca Gimbal AC, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's closed, and Deca Power's closed.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Stab/Control, all closed except the AEA.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (John now reads settings Charlie will check) Balance Couple, On.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: On Hi Mult, Landing Radar/Computer; Monitor the PGNS.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Orion, you can configure for normal RCS configuration now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We got an RCS A Reg light when that went on.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We are clear down to 5 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Based on the (garbled), it will probably be double at first (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The old Earth is sure pretty.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Orion, bring battery 3 on (line) at minus 5.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) (Garbled) pull me right up under my - little (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Five minutes, Landing Radar breaker is going closed.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Altitude Transmitter is 3.4, almost; Velocity Transmitter is 3.82.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Say again the reading on the velocity, John.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Not yet having heard Jim) Okay; Altitude Transmitter.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: My watch is set and wound.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Starting at about 10 (nautical) miles, it looks like.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Next thing is at 30 seconds, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You want us to turn the ranging back on?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: 30 seconds, Engine Arm goes to Descent, then we ullage.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If no ignition, just Start Pushbutton if we get ullage.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Descent Engine Command Override is on.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Descent Engine Command Override; Master Arm, Off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Stand by for throttle up; thrust-to-weight is okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They (meaning the flight dynamics analysts in Houston) were right on.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, Jim; at pitchover, do you want me to go Aft omni or stay Forward?.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Stay Forward and you've got a Go at 1 (minute into the burn).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we're way high, John; we got to get down.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Garbled) [Journal Contributor Paul Fjeld notes that the ability to update the LM targeting "was added on Apollo 12.
DLAND - Noun 69 - was an unusual vector!
It was added to the Position of the landing site (RLS)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And you've a Go for enter...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're going to have to yaw out here at 3.
(Pause) Might take that out when we get (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: AGS and PGNS are tracking right on, Jim!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Within a tenth of a foot a second.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Pressures are holding good in the DPS (Descent Propulsion System, pronounced "dips").
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll check the ED (Explosive Device) batteries one more time.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I say, there's no way to get the Altitude light at this height.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Altitude and Velocity lights are out at 50k!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: When do you want to accept it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, you have a Go to accept.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Six minutes, we should be at 32,000 (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Not quite back on profile, but almost.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: AGS and PGNS H-Dot are right on.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (The AGS is) still about 1000 (feet) high, looks like.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, it's starting to look pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Setting up ready...(Getting his tongue untied) (Getting) the AGS ready (for an update) at 14k.
Then I do a 360 and then turn the (16mm movie) camera on.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It was right on time, wasn't it?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You were clipping a little bit, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I can see the landing site from here, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Long Pause) How does it (meaning the targeting) look, to you?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, standing by to update the
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We got about a 3-degree roll command in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Enter, 360, minus 0172, 0 Enter, 367 is coming up, and I'm starting the clock...(correcting himself) I mean the (16-mm movie) camera.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Coming down at 182 (feet per second), a little steep.
(Pause) Okay, we're going to be right on.
It's just about right on, maybe 10 feet (feet per second off).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Looking out his window to the north) Palmetto and Dot; North Ray.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Looks like we're going to be able to make it, John.
There's not too many blocks up there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Two (LPD clicks) to the south, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's going to be a little past (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A little more to the left (south).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: 54 LPD; dropping out the bottom now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're going to be just a little long...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...but we're just now abeam of Double Spot.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Some big blocks over here to the left, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Give me a couple of clicks up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Perfect place over here, John, a couple of big boulders.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause while they drop to the surface) Boom.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, we don't have to walk far to pick up rocks, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Old Orion is finally here, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I can look right out to the left and see...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Responding to Irwin; the comm improves dramatically) Okay, you got it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're forward and to the north of Double Spot.
I would guess about 200 meters to the north and maybe 150 meters to the west.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...of Double Spot and about 150 meters west.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Man, I could see the...all the way to the ground.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To himself, checking a gauge) That's good.
Okay, Ascent Helium Monitor, cycle; I did.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Percy Precision (meaning John) has planted one on the plains of Descartes!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I better go easy on this Landing Radar circuit breaker, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, don't...Okay, that's the right one.
Hey, it sure ain't flat, John.
There's that ridge to the north.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: All we got to do is jump out the hatch and we got plenty of rocks.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Houston, boy, it sure looks like you could make - I see Crown Crater (near the summit of Stone Mountain) from here; I can see (South) Ray Crater from here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I almost had apoplexy, that Program alarm, and that's your radar breaker.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie's about had...(Laughs and changes the subject) Charlie's got nothing but a ridge to look at.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: There's a ridge out in front (stops to listen to Irwin) There's a ridge out in front of us, too, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: There's a ridge in front of us, one to the side of us, and my guess is that we're in a subdued old crater that's got a lot more craters (in it).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Jim, this ridge in front of us does look like a subdued crater and it may be the raised rim about 50 meters in front of us, about, oh, 4 or 5 meters tall.
About 30 or 40 percent of the surface is covered with boulders that are maybe half a meter in size.
On out in front of us and to the right, where we landed, there were...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Mission Control) We gonna stay, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Long Pause) Everything's looking okay up to this point, John.
We'll give you a final word here shortly.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We were coming down pretty good until I hit the Stop button, and then it fell out.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I know exactly what you mean.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's really nice to have your shadow out there; that's a good help.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The way these blocks are laid in here, out my window, I'd guess they come from South Ray.
We've got, right out in front of us about 100 meters, at my 10:30 position (that is, southeast of the LM), I've got one that must be 3 meters across.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: P68, John, and I'll get the AGS going.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, Jim, hats off and a case of beer to FIDO (Flight Dynamics Officer).
I'll tell you, that targeting was just beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You guys just started us right in there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Charlie) (Garbled) where it says we are.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Jim, our Noun 43s are minus 896...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Reading) Tig (time-of-ignition) time for T-2 is 104 (pause) plus 42 plus 16.64.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; (Reading) "PGNS Mode Control, Noun 33", you got.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Boy, this is really a nice place!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: My restraint harness has got me anchored.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Oh, that's what the trouble is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) John, that was a sup(erb landing)!
(Long Pause) It's just about level (that is, the LM is nearly vertical), we're not going to have a bit of trouble getting out...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, it's going to be neat.
But it sure is not...It's not a smooth...It's not that FS smooth, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's not that FS smooth; we're in the middle of a block field.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Looking out John's window to the south) There's Crown Crater up there (near the summit of Stone Mountain).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We may have squashed a few.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Jim, we got Crown Crater out John's left window (at) about 9 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And just looking at it from here, I don't think the Rover's going to have any trouble going up that hill.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It looked good going (to) North Ray, too.
There were some big blocks on the rim but not...The track just looked good.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Turn on the water for a second, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That shadow is not as long as I thought it was going to be.
It looks like we're right on top of the ground.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, we didn't drop very far.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Man, we got a lot of rocks, that's for sure.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Houston) How's it (meaning the LM) looking, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I wish I could tell you what kind of rocks those are Houston.
But some of them are very white; and, doggone, if I could see...I'm not close enough to them, but...And I see one white one with some black...Can't tell whether that's dirt or not on it.
But it could be a white breccia, if you believe such a thing.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Every one of them are (sic) angular, too, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They're out of South Ray, I believe.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: There's a pretty one over there, without any dust on it at all.
Out about 50 meters, by those three little craters?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Right (Pause) In fact, Houston, when I told you that I thought this terrain might be very spectacular; boy, I was just kidding.
It really is something looking at that mountain.
That is a big mountain, Charlie!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, we're within 2 minutes (of T-2), John.
It is really (spectacular) (Pause) Is that South Ray?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; let's go to P00, and then ICS PTT (Push-to-Talk).
transcript.speaker: Duke
